# Generated by iptables-save v1.8.9 on Tue May 19 11:00:00 2026
# Showcases shadowed-rule detection — each commented case fires one finding.
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
# Case 1: same action, narrower source — second rule never fires.
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -j ACCEPT
# Case 2: different action (ACCEPT then DROP). The DROP of the same traffic
# never executes — likely a misunderstanding by whoever added it.
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -s 203.0.113.0/24 -j DROP
# Case 3: range subset — 8443 falls inside 8000-9000.
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8000:9000 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8443 -j ACCEPT
# Case 4: catch-all DROP shadows every later rule.
-A INPUT -j DROP
-A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
COMMIT
